message:
fix(storage): validate issue on update path, not just create

\`SqliteStorage::create_issue\` already ran an \`IssueValidator::
validate\` pass on the incoming issue before the INSERT, rejecting
empty titles, negative estimated_minutes, etc. with a
validation-error list. \`update_issue\` did not — it applied an
\`IssueUpdate\` patch on top of the existing row and wrote the
result without re-checking any invariant. That meant a caller could
overwrite a perfectly-valid issue with a title of \"   \" or with
\`estimated_minutes = -1\` and the row would persist in the DB,
polluting downstream reads and every JSONL export.

Fix in \`update_issue\`:

- After applying patches, stamp \`updated_at = Utc::now()\` onto
  the in-memory \`issue\` struct BEFORE validation so the value
  about to hit the DB is exactly what the validator sees.
- Call \`IssueValidator::validate(&issue)\` and short-circuit with
  \`BeadsError::from_validation_errors\` on failure, matching the
  error shape \`create_issue\` already produces for the same class
  of mistakes.
- Reuse the stamped \`updated_at\` when building the final
  \`set_clauses\` / \`params\` vectors so the DB, the in-memory
  view, and the content hash are all computed off the same
  timestamp. The prior code computed \`Utc::now()\` twice, which
  was harmless but invited future refactors to introduce subtle
  skew.

Tests added in \`tests/storage_crud.rs\`:

- \`update_issue_rejects_empty_title_without_persisting\`: creates
  a valid issue, submits an update with \`title = Some(\"   \")\`,
  asserts the call returns an error mentioning \`\"title\"\`, and
  re-reads the issue to assert the persisted title was NOT
  overwritten with the whitespace string. This is the explicit
  \"failed validation must be atomic\" property.

- \`update_issue_rejects_negative_estimate_without_persisting\`:
  same shape for \`estimated_minutes = Some(Some(-1))\` (an
  explicit set to negative, not the \`None\` \"leave unchanged\"
  or \`Some(None)\` \"clear\" forms). Asserts the error mentions
  \`\"estimated_minutes\"\` and the persisted estimate equals the
  original fixture value.

Pure correctness fix. Callers that were already passing
validation data are unaffected.
